codes.EAS: Banks who eased the corresponding concept
codes.TIG: Banks who tightened the corresponding concept
codes.UNC: Banks who did not change the corresponding concept

codes.AVEL: Risk weighted assets of which Average loans
codes.BFW: Financial situation - ability to raise funding in wholesale markets
codes.BICP: Financial situation - ability to improve its capital position
codes.BIP: Financial situation - ability to improve its profitability
codes.BSC: Impact of cost of funds and balance sheet constraints
codes.CBCO: Costs related to balance sheet clean-up operations
codes.CGSA: Capital gains/losses out of sales of marketable assets
codes.COHL: Cost of holding liquidity due to negative ECB deposit facility rate
codes.CPED: Purchasing assets - corporate bonds denominated in euro
codes.CPRE: Capital position of which Retained earnings
codes.CRD: Impact of the introduction of the more risk-sensitive Basel II-based capital adequacy framework (Directive 2006/48/EC; CRD henceforth), via the potential impact on the bank capital position
codes.CRE: Impact of consumption expenditure (mortgage equity withdrawal)
codes.DEPS: Increased deposits from enterprises and households
codes.EAMA: Purchasing assets - euro area marketable assets
codes.EASB: Purchasing assets - sovereign bonds issued by other euro area member states
codes.FCON: Funding constraints (no alternative sources of financing available to your bank)
codes.FDIF: Funding difficulties (financing via financial markets only available at high prices)
codes.GLI: Impact of general level of interest rates
codes.HTNX: Services (H to N eXcluding financial services and real estate)
codes.IFO: Impact of industry or firm specific situation
codes.IFS: Impact of internal finance out of savings
codes.INV: Impact of inventories and working capital
codes.JMNZ: Other services (Administration, scientific/technical, information and social)
codes.LIQA: Total assets of which liquid assets
codes.LOB: Impact of loans from other banks
codes.LVR: Impact of loan to value ratio
codes.MAA: Impact of mergers and acquisitions and corporate restructuring
codes.MF: Impact of ability to access market financing
codes.MLTD: Refinancing - substituting maturing medium to long-term debt
codes.NEMA: Purchasing assets - non-euro area marketable assets
codes.NFCO: No funding constraints or comfortable liquidity position
codes.NSMF: Funding sought via ABSPP and/or CBPP
codes.OSF: Impact of other sources of finance
codes.OT1: First other factor for terms and conditions (see OBS_COM)
codes.OT2: Second other factor for terms and conditions (see OBS_COM)
codes.OT3: Third other factor for terms and conditions (see OBS_COM)
codes.PHRI: Investment into the reduction of physical risk
codes.PRAC: Profitability reasons linked to attractive conditions
codes.PRMO: Precautionary motive (to secure financing given the uncertainty on financial markets)
codes.PSR: Pressure related to supervisory or regulatory requirements
codes.QUES: A questionnaire was received from that bank (1=yes, else=no)
codes.RCD: Impact of risk on the collateral demanded
codes.REGR: Fulfilment of regulatory and supervisory requirements
codes.RFR: Impact of regulatory and fiscal regime
codes.RSKL: Risk weighted assets of which Riskier loans
codes.SCW: Sovereign collateral available for wholesale market transactions
codes.SDC: Impact of spending on durable consumer goods
codes.SELO: Refinancing - substituting other Eurosystem liquidity operations
codes.SNRT: Synthetic securitisation without significant risk transfer
codes.SRT: Synthetic securitisation with significant risk transfer
codes.TCON: Less attractive TLTRO conditions compared with market funding conditions
codes.TNRT: Traditional securitisation without significant risk transfer
codes.TOIB: Impact of the 2007-08 turmoil in the credit markets
codes.TOIL: Impact of financial and sovereign debt crisis 2009 onwards
codes.TRT: Traditional securitisation with significant risk transfer
codes.TTRO: Impact of TLTRO - refers to TLTRO I (BLS rounds 2014Q4 - 2016Q1), TLTRO II (BLS rounds 2016Q3 - 2017Q3) and TLTRO III (BLS rounds 2020Q1 onwards)
codes.UNRR: Reduction uncertainty due to fulfilment regulatory requirements

codes.SIDF: Supranational institutions and public development funds

codes.ALM: Money markets, debt securities or other markets
codes.CLO: Free up capital to grant new loans
codes.CPR: Free up capital to increase payout ratios
codes.DSM: Debt securities - medium to long term (including covered bonds)
codes.DSS: Debt securities - short term (e.g. certificates of deposit or commercial paper)
codes.GMR: Government announcement of recapitalisation support and state guarantees for debt securities
codes.H00: Loans secured by real estate and for purchase of second homes or homes for investment purposes
codes.MAA: M and A and corporate restructuring
codes.MMS: Interbank unsecured money market - short-term (more than one week)
codes.MMV: Interbank unsecured money market - very short term (up to one week)
codes.REGR: Fulfilment of regulatory and supervisory requirements
codes.SHL: Securitisation of loans for house purchase
codes.TCR: Ability to transfer credit risk off balance sheet
codes.X00: Loans secured by real estate and purpose other than purchase of a home for oneself

codes.B2: Backward looking 21 December 2011 to ad-hoc round

codes.BM: CRR/CRD IV, effect on credit margins
codes.CW: Cost of lending and willingness to lend
codes.DC: Sovereign debt crisis, effect on credit standards
codes.DG: Demand for loans with COVID-19 related government guarantees
codes.DM: Sovereign debt crisis, effect on credit margins
codes.DW: Demand for loans without government guarantees
codes.LA: Expanded asset purchase programme - Increased liquidity resulting from bank`s sales of marketable assets
codes.LD: Expanded asset purchase programme - Increased liquidity owing to an increase in customer deposits from enterprises and households
codes.REGR: Fulfilment of regulatory and supervisory requirements
codes.SG: Credit standards on loans on loans with COVID-19 related government guarantees
codes.SW: Credit standards on loans without government guarantees
codes.TG: Credit terms and conditions on loans with COVID-19 related government guarantees
codes.TW: Credit terms and conditions on loans without government guarantees

codes.BA: Average, weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BDINX: Diffusion index weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BF1: Frequency of 1 responses (considerably tightened / strongly negative), weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BF2: Frequency of 2 responses (somewhat tightened / negative), weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BF3: Frequency of 3 responses (basically unchanged / o), weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BF4: Frequency of 4 responses (somewhat eased / positive), weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BF5: Frequency of 5 responses (considerably eased / strongly positive), weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BF6: Frequency of 6 responses (tightest level), weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BF7: Frequency of 7 responses (level remained constant), weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BF8: Frequency of 8 responses (loosest level), weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BFNA: Frequency of not applicable responses, weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BFNB: Frequency of missing answers, weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BFNET: Weighted net percentage (tightened minus eased or reverse), based on the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BFY: Frequency of YES responses (number of YES responses divided by the sum of the sum of all answers and the sum of NA answers) weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BSD: Standard deviation estimate, weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BWA: Weighted average, based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate, also weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BWDINX: Weighted diffusion index based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate, also weighted with the share of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the banks in the BLS sample
codes.BWF1: Weighted avg frequency of 1 responses (considerably tightened / strongly negative) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWF2: Weighted avg frequency of 2 responses (somewhat tightened / negative) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWF3: Weighted avg frequency of 3 responses (basically unchanged / o) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWF4: Weighted avg frequency of 4 responses (somewhat eased / positive) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWF5: Weighted avg frequency of 5 responses (considerably eased / strongly positive) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWF6: Weighted avg frequency of 6 responses (tightest level) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWF7: Weighted avg frequency of 7 responses (level remained constant) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWF8: Weighted avg frequency of 8 responses (loosest level) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWFNA: Weighted avg frequency of not applicable responses, based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWFNB: Weighted avg frequency of missing answers, based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWFNET: Weighted net percentage (tightened minus eased or reverse) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWFY: Weighted average frequency of YES responses based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.BWSD: Weighted standard deviation estimate, based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate and of each bank in the total loan outstanding amount of the BLS banks sample
codes.F: Frequency of responses in range from 1 to 5
codes.F1: Frequency of 1 responses (considerably tightened / strongly negative / not participated / will not participate)
codes.F3: Frequency of 3 responses (basically unchanged / o / undecided)
codes.F5: Frequency of 5 responses (considerably eased / strongly positive / participated / will participate)
codes.FNET: Net percentage (frequency of tightened minus that of eased or reverse)
codes.FY: Frequency of YES responses (number of YES responses divided by the sum of the sum of all answers and the sum of NA answers)
codes.N: Number of numeric responses in range from 1 to 5
codes.N1: Number of 1 responses (considerably tightened / strongly negative)
codes.N5: Number of 5 responses (considerably eased / strongly positive)
codes.NNA: Number of factor not applicable responses (observation value NC with observation status L)
codes.NNB: Number of no such business responses (observation value NC with observation status M)
codes.NY: Number of YES responses (sum of answers per country)
codes.P1: Proportion of 1 responses (considerably tightened / strongly negative)
codes.P5: Proportion of 5 responses (considerably eased / strongly positive)
codes.PY: Proportion of YES responses (number of YES responses divided by the sum of all answers)
codes.WDINX: Weighted diffusion index based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate
codes.WF: Weighted avg frequency of responses in range from -2 to 2
codes.WF1: Weighted avg frequency of 1 responses (considerably tightened / strongly negative / not participated / will not participate)
codes.WF2: Weighted avg frequency of 2 responses (somewhat tightened / negative)
codes.WF3: Weighted avg frequency of 3 responses (basically unchanged / o / undecided)
codes.WF4: Weighted avg frequency of 4 responses (somewhat eased / positive)
codes.WF5: Weighted avg frequency of 5 responses (considerably eased / strongly positive / participated / will participate)
codes.WF6: Weighted avg frequency of 6 responses (tightest level)
codes.WF7: Weighted avg frequency of 7 responses (level remained constant)
codes.WF8: Weighted avg frequency of 8 responses (loosest level)
codes.WFNA: Weighted avg frequency of not applicable responses
codes.WFNB: Weighted avg frequency of missing answers
codes.WFNET: Weighted net percentage (tightened minus eased or reverse) based on the share of each country in the total loan outstanding amounts of the area aggregate
codes.WFY: Weighted avg frequency of YES responses
codes.WP1: Weighted avg proportion of 1 responses (considerably tightened / strongly negative)
codes.WP2: Weighted avg proportion of 2 responses (somewhat tightened / negative)
codes.WP3: Weighted avg proportion of 3 responses (basically unchanged / o)
codes.WP4: Weighted avg proportion of 4 responses (somewhat eased / positive)
codes.WP5: Weighted avg proportion of 5 responses (considerably eased / strongly positive)
codes.WP6: Weighted avg proportion of 6 responses (tightest level)
codes.WP7: Weighted avg proportion of 7 responses (level remained constant)
codes.WP8: Weighted avg proportion of 8 responses (loosest level)
codes.WPY: Weighted avg proportion of YES responses
